name: Okra Rice with Chicken and Mole Orange Sauce
recipeIngredient: 4 ounces brown rice (e.g., Uncle Ben's Chicken and Herb)
1 teaspoon Butter Buds®, dissolved in 1 tablespoon hot water
5 ounces boned and skinned chicken breast halves
recipeInstructions:
1. Warm a saucepan and roast the almonds for about 3 minutes.
2. Add rice mix with seasoning package, additional instant rice, water, dissolved butter substitute (or diet margarine). Bring to a boil; reduce heat, add green onions, cover, and simmer about 15 minutes.
3. Add sliced okra so it's covered by the rice and its cooking liquid. Season with Cre-ole' Spice Blend. Continue cooking for another 7 minutes; adjust heat as necessary to puff the rice without overcooking the okra.
4. Juice one orange and stir in commercial Mole paste made with ancho peppers, etc. Set aside.
5. Slice chicken breasts into 4 medallion-sized pieces. Heat a ridged griddle or skillet over high. Remove pan from heat, lightly spray surface, return to burner, and sear chicken well on one side (about 3 minutes). Sear the other side for 1-1/2 minutes.
6. Add orange juice and mole mixture; reduce liquid to sauce. Occasionally ladle sauce over meat to keep it moist. Sprinkle with chopped cilantro to taste.
7. Remove rice mixture from heat, add chopped cilantro, stir in yogurt and grated parmesan cheese; season to taste.
8. Serve rice alongside chicken; drizzle chicken with sauce. Puddle some sauce on the plate for dipping tortillas. Garnish with lime slices, additional cilantro, fresh pepper. Pair with a plain crisp salad and fresh lime.
